title: St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF
tagline: St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF — spiritual town of Maharashtra

heroImage.alt: St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF, Karnataka

overview.short: St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF is a much-loved spiritual town in Maharashtra, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Michael's and All Angels' Church is located at Oorgaum, Kolar Gold Fields, India. The church stands on Cooke Road, near the KGF Club, south of the Catholic Mother of Mines Church. The origin of the church goes back to 1899, and was for the exclusive use of the (white) officers of the John Taylor and Sons, London, which owned the gold mines at Kolar Gold Fields (KGF), Mysore State.

topPlaces.name: Particle experiments at Kolar Gold Fields
topPlaces.description: The Kolar Gold Fields (KGF), located in the Kolar district of the state of Karnataka, India, are a set of defunct gold mines known for the neutrino particle experiments and unusual observations that took place there starting in 1960. The experiments ended with the closing of the mine in 1992.

image.alt: Particle experiments at Kolar Gold Fields

topPlaces.name: Coromandel, KGF
topPlaces.description: Coromandel is a mining area in Kolar Gold Fields(KGF), Karnataka, India. Where major mining units are located.

topPlaces.name: Kolar Gold Fields
topPlaces.description: Kolar Gold Fields (K.G.F.) is a mining region in K.G.F. taluk (township), Kolar district, Karnataka, India. It is headquartered in Robertsonpet, where employees of Bharat Gold Mines Limited (BGML) and BEML Limited (formerly Bharat Earth Movers Limited) and their families live.

topPlaces.name: King George Hall, Kolar Gold Fields
topPlaces.description: King George Hall is a colonial-era civic building located in Robertsonpet, part of the Kolar Gold Fields (KGF) township in Karnataka, India. The hall was constructed to commemorate the Delhi Durbar of 1911, which marked the formal coronation of George V as Emperor of India and the announcement of shifting the imperial capital from Calcutta to Delhi.

image.alt: King George Hall, Kolar Gold Fields

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (65 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (74 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF at a relaxed pace.

faq.a: Stays in St. Michael's and All Angels' Church, Oorgaum, KGF start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
